message_subject: Joye double-add ladder for short Weierstrass curves in co-Z arithmetic (#1823)
message_body:
* Joye double-add ladder for short Weierstrass curves in co-Z arithmetic

* Use Z.modulo to redefine SS' as suggested by Andres and simplify proofs

* Save some loc

* More comments

* Use sigma types for the Joye ladder

* Use more sigma types and clean up

* Joye ladder: refactor SS, TT

* Simplify HordP

* Use match-goal-with to make proof more readable

* Avoid the +1-1 when defining/using SS/TT as suggested by Andres

* Refactor the ladder

---------

Co-authored-by: Alix Trieu <alix@trieu.dev>
Co-authored-by: Andres Erbsen <andres-github@andres.systems>

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Fix DCE/Subst01 to work under lambdas (#1809)
message_body:
The naive encoding of PHOAS passes that need to produce both [expr]-like output and data-like output simultaneously involves exponential blowup. This commit adds caching of results (and/or intermediates) of a data-producing PHOAS pass in a tree structure that mimics the PHOAS expression so that a subsequent pass can consume this tree and a PHOAS expression to produce a new expression. More concretely, suppose we are trying to write a pass that is `expr var1 * expr var2 -> A * expr var3`. We can define an `expr`-like-tree-structure that (a) doesn't use higher-order things for `Abs` nodes, and (b) stores `A` at every node. Then we can write a pass that is `expr var1 * expr var2 -> A * tree-of-A` and then `expr var1 * expr var2 * tree-of-A -> expr var3` such that we incur only linear overhead. See also #1761 and https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/issues/1604#issuecomment-1553341559. Fixes #1604

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: ASM Equivalence Checker: Preprare proofs for using bounds from the dag

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Make the dag available in ASM equiv rewrite passes (#1505)
message_body:
We don't currently use it, but this will allow us to augment `bound_expr` to make use of bounds in the dag and write passes that use this augmented bounds information. In theory, this may also allow us to do lazy revealing of expressions rather than needing to reveal them deeply up-front. This is split off so that it is possible to add more rewrite passes without conflicting too much with changes to how bounds are handled.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Factor FMapTrie proofs out of the module functor (#1290)
message_body:
* Factor FMapTrie proofs out of the module functor

Fixes #1284

* Revert "[TEMP] Disable FMapTrie functor instantiation (#1288)"

This reverts commit c68535d423a6e61da375a15b8365854a4203451c.

author: Dustin Jamner
message_subject: Factor broadcasting code into separate file

author: Dustin Jamner
message_subject: Initial work on 'broadcast' operations for expression-based loops

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add examples of Tries for positive, N, Z (#1274)
message_body: Hopefully it's actually efficient when extracted.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Split FMapTrie proofs and definitions for positivity checker (#1273)
message_body:
* Split FMapTrie proofs and definitions for positivity checker

* Split up the shape module

* Fix trie shape ind so that it's actually true

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
It compiles, but unfortunately the positivity checker is unhappy with
our schema for non-empty maps :-(, see
https://github.com/coq/coq/issues/16120

It's also very slow :-(
```
src/Util/FSets/FMapTrie.vo (real: 194.55, user: 193.16, sys: 1.24, mem: 818488 ko)
```

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
I've left some unused tactics in there because they may be useful
references in the future, or as a prototype for tries.

author: Dustin Jamner
message_subject: Revert "Revert commits that don't build on master. (#1221)"
message_body: This reverts commit 959e1c3f8b01f6523a7401eb66dfe42d39fcb25e.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add a bunch of iso-based FMap and MSet stuff (#1222)

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This allows us to check multiple assembly files without having to
resynthesize the PHOAS input, as suggested in
https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/issues/1170#issuecomment-1076886198.

What remains is to update the Makefile generation to take advantage of
this.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Finish almost all of the proof of combine_consts

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Parameterize over dereference_scalar in more places
message_body:
This will allow us to reuse the same code to load inputs and outputs,
when we want to enforce the spec of reading back and removing all of the
values and then saying that the resulting symbolic memory state is
empty.

Some of the proofs are not quite as complete as they were before, but I
hope to replace them soon with a more principled approach.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Split off the non-bedrock-dependent part of equiv proofs
message_body:
This will hopefully make development a bit faster; these files are so
long.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add some lemmas about Forall2 and concat

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
All the lemmas in Coq.Lists.List and Coq.Sorting.Permutation present in
current master (8.16?) but not in 8.11.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Minor progress towards finishing asm equiv proof
message_body: The way that we're dealing with load state needs to be rethought :-(

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: More progress on assembly equivalence proof
message_body:
The only thing that remains to be proven is that the runtime output
values that we get are the ones we expect to get.  I need to figure out
where to propogate this information from, but this proof is almost done!

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Finish proving/using that SymexLines scribles only over known memory
message_body:
What remains is doing the reverse of the final symex goal to deal with
the different representations of memory specs, splitting apart input and
output values via `++` (not 100% sure how to do this yet), and showing
that the outputs are mapped to the right values (also need to figure out
how to do this).

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Update asm equiv to finish all but output goal

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
* Add `encode_word` and `copy` to PushButtonSynthesis

* Add copy_correct

* Fix unfolding

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Some more work on equivalence-checking assembly proofs (#1093)
message_body:
Also, factor out build_merge_array_addresses

Co-authored-by: Andres Erbsen <andreser@mit.edu>

Co-authored-by: Andres Erbsen <andreser@mit.edu>

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add various utility lemmas and definitions (#1100)
message_body:
* Add various utility lemmas and definitions

Including `inversion_list`, `inversion_pair`, `inversion_nat_rel`,
`inversion_nat_eq`.

* Fix for Coq 8.12

author: Jason Gross
message_body: Now we rely less on the particular form of the operation for most proofs

message_subject: Push more things through asm proofs

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
We now default to avoiding uint1 in the result of `>>` operations, as
per
https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/pull/1015#discussion_r708691741.

This is required for making symbolic equivalence checking with assembly
go through.  It might be possible to improve the symbolic equivalence
checker to know enough about bounds analysis (or to also do bounds
analysis) so that this is not necessary?

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
We now compute the loose bounds by recording the number of additions
and/or balanced subtractions we want to be able to do in a row before we
need to multiply and carry.  Note that balanced subtraction always takes
a bit more overhead than addition.

This is a more conservative variant of #799 that doesn't actually change
the bounds, and makes the genuine change of #799 easier to see, namely,
reducing `headspace_add_count` from `2` to `1`.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add support for comments and stripping annotations
message_body:
After `Import Crypto.Language.PreExtra`, it's now possible to do `dlet _
:= ident.comment stuff in ...` and have `stuff` appear in the
synthesized code as a comment.  There's room for improvement with
regards to quoting and handling multi-line comments, but it works for
debugging purposes.

Note that bounds analysis does not add casts underneath comments, though
I believe let-lifting still happens, and the comments are still
definitely subject to rewriting.

If you want comments whose variable dependencies are eliminated during
the very last dead-code-elimination phase of the stringification
pipeline, use `ident.comment_no_keep`.  Both `ident.comment` and
`ident.comment_no_keep` have type `forall {A}, A -> unit`.

There are now also passes `StripAnnotations` and `StripAllAnnotations`;
the former takes an expression and input bounds, and strips cast
annotations which are guaranteed to be no-ops given the bounds; it's
proven to preserve `Wf` and `Interp`-relatedness.  The
`StripAllAnnotations` pass strips *all* cast annotations, even the ones
that might be doing necessary truncation or modulo computations, and
does not require input bounds.  It's proven to preserve `Wf` but has no
`Interp` proofs.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This is in preparation for autogenerating most of Identifier.v

To allow most of the code to continue compiling without much
modification, all of the qualified names we used to have in Identifier.v
now exist as only-parsing notations in API.v.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Factor the Rewriter over the type of identifiers and base types

message_body:
Since more of the definitions are now done in Gallina, hopefully these
should be easier to factor out / parameterize over the type of
identifiers, now.

```diff
            | Compilers.Coq_ident.Coq_fancy_selc -> None
            | Compilers.Coq_ident.Coq_fancy_selm _ -> None
            | Compilers.Coq_ident.Coq_fancy_sell -> None
```

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Reduce more in mulmod and squaremod
message_body:
After some debugging with Andres, the issue seems to be that when we
have multiple TAPs, the first reduction will leave some of the partial
products past the end of the table, because it "folds over" the partial
products table at the location of each TAP.  In the worst case, if we
have a TAP at 1 and a TAP at the second-highest limb, we will have to
reduce as many times as there are limbs.  To prevent quadratic blowup,
we short-circuit the repeat_reduce calculation whenever the list of high
terms is nil.  We deliberately do not support primes with a TAP in the
highest limb.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Switch to a more precise version of interp_related for rewrites
message_body:
This version is hopefully the right one.  It seems to be basically
exactly what we want to say, and no more and no less.  The rule for
let-ins, which is almost the only freedom we have here, is chosen to
match exactly with the rule for App+Abs.  Note that using Leibniz
equality is essential in the places where it's used.  The recursive
relation here might actually be a bit clearer as an inductive relation,
but I wanted it to simplify under reduction.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
It now lives in Arithmetic.Core.B.Positional, where it belongs, rather
than in Specific/.../HelperTactics.

Andres notes that we probably don't need this at all, and could instead
make chained_carries reduce after every index (and the spurious
reductions should be no-ops).  I didn't want to bother verifying this,
at the moment, so I left it as-is.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Factor out some of the preglue synthesis code
message_body:
This makes it a bit more uniform, and hopefully more automatable and
packageable.  Unfortunately, there's still no spec for this part of the
pipeline, so the tactics simply aggregate common patterns.

Alas, this also makes things a bit slower; I suspect that [Defined] is
the place where things are slower.

author: Jason Gross
message_body: Now it can handle ops that return (Tbase TZ)

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Push bounds side conditions through the pipeline
message_body: This will (hopefully) be useful for karatsuba.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Fix loop notations, add for loops

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This fixes all of the private-names warnings emitted by
compiling fiat-crypto with https://github.com/coq/coq/pull/268 (minus
the ones in coqprime, which I didn't touch).

author: Jason Gross
message_body: Written via pair-programming with Andres.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This closes #171.

It's unfortunately a bit fragile, and takes a really long time (about
60s) to prove correct, because Coq is bad at deep dependent pattern
matching.

We enable a-normal form for the freeze test, because the rewriter only
works when the arguments to adc are var or const.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add reflective machinery for adc, zselect

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This one is much easier to compute with, and it means we can get rid of
a lot of pesky Named.wff proofs.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add loop invariant framework for for-loops
message_body: Also fix bugs in for-loop definition to make the proofs go through

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
It's now more amenable to use in the montgomery loop, and the display
will only have one copy of the code.

Unfortunately, it's much more ad-hoc and kludgy now.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add initial version of CSE interp

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Finish most of wf for CSE
message_body: Only thing left is figuring out how to handle the [prefix]

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Rework second 2/3 of the glue: work on ladderstep
message_body:
The first part of the glue part of the pipeline still doesn't handle
maps, but it will shortly.  We also now have a much more precise goal
shape checker, and much nicer and more informative error messages when
the shape doesn't match.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This closes #146 and makes `make quick` faster.

The changes were generated by adding [Global Set Suggest Proof Using.]
to GlobalSettings.v, and then following [the instructions for a script I
wrote](https://github.com/JasonGross/coq-tools#proof-using-helper).

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This is in preparation for dropping extra carries.  What remains to be
done, after this and #106, is to finish packaging up the reified
[add_coordinates] so that it can operate on [Tuple.tuple
GF25519BoundedCommon.fe25519 4], and then to prove

```coq
forall twice_d P1 P2, Tuple.fieldwise GF25519BoundedCommon.eq
  (<reflected add_coordinates> twice_d P1 P2)
  (@ExtendedCoordinates.Extended.add_coordinates GF25519BoundedCommon.fe25519
        GF25519Bounded.add GF25519Bounded.sub GF25519Bounded.mul twice_d
   P1 P2)
```

I'm not sure how to do this, or even what the right structure for the
proof is.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: First pass at bounded version of GF25519
message_body:
Boundedness is axiomatized as
```coq
Axiom ExprBinOp : Type.
Axiom ExprUnOp : Type.
Axiom interp_bexpr : ExprBinOp -> Specific.GF25519.fe25519 ->
Specific.GF25519.fe25519 -> Specific.GF25519.fe25519.
Axiom interp_uexpr : ExprUnOp -> Specific.GF25519.fe25519 ->
Specific.GF25519.fe25519.
Axiom radd : ExprBinOp.
Axiom rsub : ExprBinOp.
Axiom rmul : ExprBinOp.
Axiom ropp : ExprUnOp.
Axiom rinv : ExprUnOp.
Axiom radd_correct : forall x y, interp_bexpr radd x y = carry_add x y.
Axiom rsub_correct : forall x y, interp_bexpr rsub x y = carry_sub x y.
Axiom rmul_correct : forall x y, interp_bexpr rmul x y = mul x y.
Axiom ropp_correct : forall x, interp_uexpr ropp x = carry_opp x.
Axiom rinv_correct : forall x, interp_uexpr rinv x = inv x.
Axiom check_bbounds : ExprBinOp -> bool.
Axiom check_ubounds : ExprUnOp -> bool.
Axiom radd_bounded : check_bbounds radd = true.
Axiom rsub_bounded : check_bbounds rsub = true.
Axiom rmul_bounded : check_bbounds rmul = true.
Axiom ropp_bounded : check_ubounds ropp = true.
Axiom rinv_bounded : check_ubounds rinv = true.
Axiom bbounds_correct
  : forall rexpr, check_bbounds rexpr = true
                  -> forall x y, is_bounded x = true
                                 -> is_bounded y = true
                                 -> is_bounded (interp_bexpr rexpr x y)
= true.
Axiom ubounds_correct
  : forall rexpr, check_ubounds rexpr = true
                  -> forall x, is_bounded x = true
                               -> is_bounded (interp_uexpr rexpr x) =
true.
```

Questions:
- Some of this is very general and probably doesn't belong in this file
  (e.g., [correct_le_le]).  Should it stay, or move elsewhere?
- There's a lot of type munging that could be generalized to arbitrary
  heterogenous lists; should it be generalized?
- How do we do [div] on bounded things?  Currently, I have
```coq
Axiom div : forall (f g : fe25519), fe25519.
Axiom div_correct : forall a b : fe25519,
    ModularBaseSystem.eq (proj1_fe25519 (div a b))
                         (ModularBaseSystem.div (proj1_fe25519 a)
(proj1_fe25519 b)).
```

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
By being careful about building the expressions in the first place, we
no longer need it, and can rely on dead code elimination.

author: jadep
message_subject: Move side lemmas to appropriate files

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add correctness of interpretation of linearize and inline_const

author: Jason Gross
message_body: The proof of wf-preservation for inline_const isn't finished yet, though...

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Fix order of binders, and add WfProofs

author: jadep
message_subject: Defined an equality comparison for tuples that uses bool instead of Prop (like Z.eqb). This is necessary for the runtime equality comparison on tuples that will appear in square root calculations during point-decoding.

author: Robert Sloan
message_subject: Removed nat arguments to Evaluable operations

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Now the rewrite strategy no longer relies on projections of anything
other than [decode], and the conversion to ZLike is simpler.

Modulo some annoyingly delicate arithmetic around things like [2^n * 2^n
= 2^(2*n)] and whether to factor [(decode (fst x) + decode (snd x) >> n)
>> b] as [decode x >> n] or as [shrd (fst x) (snd x) n], the proofs
bascially go by pulling/pushing decodes.
